generations.code:
import sys
from itertools import product

input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s.append(input().strip())
    
    w = []
    for j in range(m):
        col = [int(s[i][j]) for i in range(n)]
        w.append(col)
    
    best_val = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for mask in range(1 << n):
        sigma = [1 if (mask >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)]
        weights = []
        for j in range(m):
            wj = sum(sigma[i] * w[j][i] for i in range(n))
            weights.append((wj, j))
        weights.sort()
        p = [0] * m
        for rank, (wj, j) in enumerate(weights):
            p[j] = rank + 1
        r = [0] * n
        for j in range(m):
            for i in range(n):
                r[i] += p[j] * w[j][i]
        val = sum(abs(x[i] - r[i]) for i in range(n))
        if val > best_val:
            best_val = val
            best_p = p[:]
    
    print(*best_p)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

bugs.description: The algorithm assigns permutation values in the wrong order: it sorts the column weights in ascending order and then gives the smallest rank to the smallest weight, which maximizes Σ σ_i * r_i instead of minimizing it. This leads to suboptimal surprise value because the optimal assignment should give the smallest p to the largest weight (i.e., sort descending or assign reverse ranks).

The algorithm assigns permutation values in the wrong order: it sorts the column weights in ascending order and then gives the smallest rank to the smallest weight, which maximizes Σ σ_i * r_i instead of minimizing it. This leads to suboptimal surprise value because the optimal assignment should give the smallest p to the largest weight (i.e., sort descending or assign reverse ranks).

The algorithm maximizes the linear expression Σ sigma_i * r_i instead of minimizing it, which is required for maximizing Σ |x_i - r_i|. Consequently, the permutation p is constructed by sorting weights in ascending order (line 27) and assigning ranks (lines 29-30), which yields the opposite of the optimal ordering. This logical error leads to incorrect results for many inputs.

The algorithm maximizes the linear expression Σ sigma_i * r_i instead of minimizing it, which is required for maximizing Σ |x_i - r_i|. Consequently, the permutation p is constructed by sorting weights in ascending order (line 27) and assigning ranks (lines 29-30), which yields the opposite of the optimal ordering. This logical error leads to incorrect results for many inputs.

The algorithm enumerates all 2^n sign assignments and for each computes weights and r using double loops over m and n, resulting in O(2^n * n * m) operations (~200 million in the worst case). This is too slow for Python and will cause TLE.

The algorithm enumerates all 2^n sign assignments and for each computes weights and r using double loops over m and n, resulting in O(2^n * n * m) operations (~200 million in the worst case). This is too slow for Python and will cause TLE.

The algorithm assigns permutation values in the wrong order: for each sign mask it sorts columns by weighted sum wj in ascending order and assigns the smallest p to the smallest wj. To maximize the objective, the sum ∑ p_j * wj should be minimized, which requires assigning the smallest p to the largest wj (i.e., sorting descending or assigning p = m - rank). This incorrect ordering leads to suboptimal permutations and therefore wrong answers.

The algorithm assigns permutation values in the wrong order: for each sign mask it sorts columns by weighted sum wj in ascending order and assigns the smallest p to the smallest wj. To maximize the objective, the sum ∑ p_j * wj should be minimized, which requires assigning the smallest p to the largest wj (i.e., sorting descending or assigning p = m - rank). This incorrect ordering leads to suboptimal permutations and therefore wrong answers.

fixes.code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s.append(input().strip())
    
    # w[j][i] = 1 if student i answered question j correctly
    w = []
    for j in range(m):
        col = tuple(int(s[i][j]) for i in range(n))
        w.append(col)
    
    best_val = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for mask in range(1 << n):
        sigma = [1 if (mask >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)]
        
        # Compute wj for each column
        weights = []
        for j in range(m):
            wj = 0
            for i in range(n):
                wj += sigma[i] * w[j][i]
            weights.append((wj, j))
        
        weights.sort()
        p = [0] * m
        for rank, (wj, j) in enumerate(weights):
            p[j] = rank + 1
        
        # Compute r[i] for each student
        r = [0] * n
        for j in range(m):
            pj = p[j]
            for i in range(n):
                r[i] += pj * w[j][i]
        
        val = sum(abs(x[i] - r[i]) for i in range(n))
        if val > best_val:
            best_val = val
            best_p = p[:]
    
    print(*best_p)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    x = list(map(int, input().split()))
    s = []
    for _ in range(n):
        s.append(input().strip())
    
    # w[j][i] = 1 if student i answered question j correctly
    w = []
    for j in range(m):
        col = tuple(int(s[i][j]) for i in range(n))
        w.append(col)
    
    best_val = -1
    best_p = None
    
    for mask in range(1 << n):
        sigma = [1 if (mask >> i) & 1 else -1 for i in range(n)]
        
        # Compute wj for each column
        weights = []
        for j in range(m):
            wj = 0
            for i in range(n):
                wj += sigma[i] * w[j][i]
            weights.append((wj, j))
        
        weights.sort()
        p = [0] * m
        for rank, (wj, j) in enumerate(weights):
            p[j] = rank + 1
        
        # Compute r[i] for each student
        r = [0] * n
        for j in range(m):
            pj = p[j]
            for i in range(n):
                r[i] += pj * w[j][i]
        
        val = sum(abs(x[i] - r[i]) for i in range(n))
        if val > best_val:
            best_val = val
            best_p = p[:]
    
    print(*best_p)

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
